revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23991619004|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Imaichi Dainenji Kofun]][[ja:今市大念寺古墳]]
{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} | name =Imaichi Dainenji Kofun | native_name =今市大念寺古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Imaichi Dainenji Kofun, entrance-1.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Imaichi Dainenji Kofun | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Imaichi Dainenji Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.363122|N|132.764764|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Izumo|qid=Q825166|1=Izumo, Shimane}} | region = {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|qid=Q398310|lt=San'in region|1=San'in region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|21|47.24|N|132|45|53.15|E|}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Imaichi Dainenji Kofun'''|今市大念寺古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Imaichi neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Izumo|en|Izumo|qid=Q301580|lt=Izumo|1=Izumo}} in the {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|qid=Q398310|lt=San'in region|1=San'in region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1924.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139114|title=今市大念寺古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is the largest {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=keyhole-shaped Kofun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} in the {{ill|Izumo Province|en|Izumo Province|qid=Q582846|lt=Izumo region|1=Izumo Province}}, with a total length of about 100 meters, and the excavated items are designated as Tangible Cultural Properties of Izumo City.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://www.city.izumo.shimane.jp/www/contents/1142485283146/|title=出雲市内の文化財|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Imaichi Dainenji Kofun is located on the edge of a hill overlooking the central part of the Izumo Plain in the eastern part of Shimane Prefecture. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus is adjacent to the {{ill|Main Hall (Japanese Buddhism)|en|Main Hall (Japanese Buddhism)|qid=Q17051413|lt=Main Hall|1=Main Hall (Japanese Buddhism)}} of Dainen-ji, a Buddhist temple founded in 1452, and the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] of the tumulus was opened when the temple expanded its Main Hall in 1826. Modern [[archaeological excavation]]s were conducted from 1981 to 1983. The north side was severely damaged due to the construction of the hall, leaving a current total length of 92 meters. The posterior circular portion has a diameter of 45 meters and a height of seven meters. Cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' were found on the surface, but there is no evidence of ''[[fukiishi]]''. The tumulus was built using rammed earth, which is rare for a ''kofun'' Kofun. The burial chamber is a horizontal stone chamber, and the posterior circular portion opens in the west-southwest direction. Measuring 12.8 meters in total length, the burial chamber is a large multi-chamber structure consisting of a back chamber which held the stone sarcophagus, a front chamber, and a passageway. Many {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} were unearthed when the burial chamber was opened in the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}. These included gilt-bronze sandals, iron swords, spears, axes, horse harnesses, and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} earthenware. Some of these items are still preserved at Dainen-ji, but most have subsequently been lost. From these artifacts, it is estimated that the tumulus was constructed in the latter half of the 6th century in the late Kofun period. Rammed construction is a technique that appears in the [[:en:Kinai region|Kinai region]] since around the 7th century, and thus it was an extremely advanced technology at the time of the construction of this tumulus. It is speculated that there is a relationship between this progressiveness and the Hioki clan, a {{ill|Toraijin|en|Toraijin|qid=Q11561926|lt=immigrant clan|1=Toraijin}} mentioned in the "[[Fudoki|Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki]]".<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The [[Nishidani Tombs]] and the [[Kamienyatsukiyama Kofun]] are located nearby. The Imaichi Dainenji Kofun is located about one kilometer to the east of {{ill|Izumoshi Station|en|Izumoshi Station|qid=Q860288|lt=Izumoshi Station|1=Izumoshi Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|qid=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|San'in Main Line|en|San'in Main Line|qid=Q1135170|lt=San'in Main Line|1=San'in Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
<gallery> Imaichi Dainenji Kofun, 3D-1.jpg|3D rendition of the burial chamber Imaichi Dainenji Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Sarcophagus Imaichi Dainenji Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Inside burial chamber looking towards entrance </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
